phase_name: Global Hotkey Does Nothing — Root Cause Diagnosis and Fix
problem_statement: After a new `cargo build`, running `yaptap` opens the app and the menu works correctly (clicking 'Start Recording' starts recording), but pressing the global hotkey (⌥Space by default) has no visible effect: no icon change, no recording starts, no error alert appears.
root_cause_analysis: The menu-button path (MenuEvent → AppEvent::HotkeyPressed) runs entirely on the main thread and never touches the rdev listener. The hotkey path requires: (1) Accessibility permission granted for the exact binary on disk, (2) rdev::listen() successfully registering a CGEventTap at the HID level, (3) the correct key+modifier combination reaching the tap without being consumed upstream, and (4) AppEvent::HotkeyPressed delivered and processed by the main event loop. A failure in steps 1–3 is completely silent when the app is not launched from a terminal (no stderr visible). The most likely cause after a new build is that macOS requires re-granting Accessibility permission for the updated binary: unsigned binaries are identified by path+code hash and a rebuild changes the hash, voiding prior approval.

issues.title: Verify Accessibility permission is granted for the post-build binary
issues.description: macOS Accessibility access is scoped to the binary identity (path + code hash). Every `cargo build` produces a binary with a different hash. If Accessibility was approved for a prior build, the new binary may require re-approval. The app calls `ax_is_process_trusted()` once at launch (app.rs:496): if it returns false the rdev thread is never spawned, leaving the hotkey silently inactive while the menu button continues to work via the main-thread event channel. Additionally, the binary that macOS approved (e.g. ~/.cargo/bin/yaptap installed via `cargo install`) may differ from the binary actually being run (e.g. ./target/debug/yaptap from a fresh build); Accessibility permission is path-specific and each path is a separate entry.
checks: In the terminal used to launch yaptap, run: `which yaptap` (or note the explicit path if run as ./target/debug/yaptap).
Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility.
Confirm there is an entry whose path matches exactly what was found in the previous step, and that it is toggled ON.
If the entry is absent or unchecked: toggle it ON (or add the binary with the '+' button), quit YapTap, relaunch it.
Press ⌥Space after relaunch — confirm the tray icon switches to the active variant.

issues.title: Launch with RUST_LOG=debug to confirm rdev thread starts and listen() does not error
issues.description: In normal use, stderr is invisible. The rdev thread logs 'rdev thread spawned' (app.rs:513) before calling rdev::listen(). If listen() fails immediately (e.g. CGEventTap creation denied), it logs 'rdev listen error' and sends AppEvent::HotkeyError. Launching from a terminal with RUST_LOG=debug makes both log lines visible and distinguishes 'thread never started' from 'thread started but tap failed' from 'tap started but events not received'.
checks: From the project root, run: `RUST_LOG=debug ./target/debug/yaptap`
Confirm the log contains: `DEBUG yaptap::app rdev thread spawned`
Confirm there is NO subsequent: `ERROR yaptap::app rdev listen error: ...`
Press ⌥Space and confirm the log contains: `INFO yaptap::app HotkeyPressed state=Idle`
If 'rdev thread spawned' is absent: Accessibility was denied at launch (see P7-I001).
If 'rdev listen error' appears: the HotkeyError alert should appear in the UI; investigate the error string.
If HotkeyPressed never logs: events are not reaching the tap (see P7-I003, P7-I005).

issues.title: Check for a system keyboard shortcut that intercepts ⌥Space before rdev's event tap
issues.description: rdev on macOS uses kCGSessionEventTap (session level), not kCGHIDEventTap. System-level event taps registered with higher priority (e.g. for Input Sources switching or Dictation) run before session-level taps and can consume the event before rdev sees it. If ⌥Space is bound to 'Select the previous input source' or similar, the system tap consumes the KeyDown and rdev never fires the callback.
checks: Open System Settings → Keyboard → Keyboard Shortcuts → Input Sources.
Confirm neither 'Select the previous input source' nor 'Select next source in Input menu' is bound to ⌥Space.
Open System Settings → Keyboard → Keyboard Shortcuts → Spotlight — confirm Spotlight search is not ⌥Space.
Open System Settings → Keyboard → Keyboard Shortcuts → Services — scan for any ⌥Space binding.
If any conflict is found: either rebind the system shortcut or change YapTap's hotkey in ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml to a non-conflicting combo (e.g. cmd+shift+y), then restart YapTap.
Press the hotkey again and confirm P7-I002 log shows HotkeyPressed.

issues.title: Accessibility alert missing 'restart required' instruction — user may grant permission but not relaunch
issues.description: When AXIsProcessTrusted() returns false, the alert shown (app.rs:497-506) reads: 'YapTap needs Accessibility access to capture the global hotkey. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility?' The alert offers 'Open Settings' / 'Later', but neither the alert text nor any follow-up tells the user to quit and relaunch YapTap after granting. The spec (hotkey.md) explicitly states: 'Re-launch the app after granting permission to activate the hotkey.' A user who grants permission and returns to the running app will find the hotkey still inactive — because the rdev thread was never spawned at launch and is not re-attempted. Fix: append the restart instruction to the alert body.
checks: Revoke Accessibility for yaptap in System Settings, then launch yaptap.
Confirm an Accessibility alert appears.
Confirm the alert body contains text instructing the user to quit and relaunch YapTap after granting permission (e.g. 'After granting permission, quit and relaunch YapTap.').
In app.rs, update the alert message string at the show_alert() call near line 499 to include: '\n\nAfter granting permission, quit and relaunch YapTap to activate the hotkey.'
Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Re-run the alert scenario and confirm the updated text appears.

issues.title: main_key_down flag can get stuck true after a missed KeyRelease, permanently suppressing the hotkey
issues.description: The rdev thread tracks `main_key_down: bool` to suppress key-repeat events (P5-I008). It is set true on the first KeyPress(Space) when modifiers are satisfied, and cleared on KeyRelease(Space). If the KeyRelease event is missed — for example, the user presses ⌥Space while switching apps, during a menu modal loop, or during system sleep/wake — `main_key_down` stays true for the remainder of the session. Every subsequent ⌥Space press fails the `!main_key_down` guard (app.rs:541) and is silently dropped. There is no timeout, reset on focus change, or any other recovery path. Fix: reset main_key_down when the pressed set becomes empty (all keys released), which is a reliable proxy for 'no key is physically held'.
checks: In the rdev listen closure (app.rs:521-554), locate the KeyRelease branch.
After `pressed.remove(&key)`, add: `if pressed.is_empty() { main_key_down = false; }`
This ensures that when every key is released (regardless of which release event was missed), main_key_down is reset.
Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Test: press ⌥Space (starts recording) → while Space is held, open the tray menu (the modal loop may swallow the KeyRelease) → release → press ⌥Space again → confirm icon responds (stops recording).
Alternatively: press ⌥Space → before releasing, switch to another app → release → switch back → press ⌥Space → confirm the hotkey fires.

issues.title: Add tracing::info! for successful rdev event tap startup confirmation
issues.description: rdev::listen() blocks indefinitely on success and never returns until an error. 'rdev thread spawned' (app.rs:513) fires before listen() is called, so it does not confirm the CGEventTap actually registered. Adding a one-shot log inside the callback (which fires on the very first event of any type — key, mouse move, etc.) confirms the tap is live and receiving events. In Rust, a `static` item can be declared inside a function body but not inside a closure body; the Once must be declared in the thread::spawn closure scope before rdev::listen() is called, then referenced (not captured) inside the callback.
checks: In the thread::spawn closure body (app.rs, before the `let result = rdev::listen(...)` call), add: `static LISTEN_STARTED: std::sync::Once = std::sync::Once::new();`
Inside the rdev::listen() callback closure (the `move |event|` body), as the very first line, add: `LISTEN_STARTED.call_once(|| tracing::info!("rdev event tap active"));`
Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Run `RUST_LOG=info ./target/debug/yaptap`, move the mouse or press any key — confirm log contains 'rdev event tap active'.
This confirms the tap is live. If this line never appears, the callback is never being called despite rdev::listen() not returning an error (indicates a silent CGEventTap registration failure).

issues.title: End-to-end smoke test: ⌥Space starts recording, second ⌥Space stops and places transcript on clipboard
issues.description: After P7-I001 through P7-I009 are resolved (or confirmed not applicable), run a full end-to-end verification that the global hotkey path works correctly from keypress to clipboard output.
checks: Run `RUST_LOG=info ./target/debug/yaptap` from the project root.
Confirm the tray icon appears (idle variant).
Press ⌥Space — confirm the tray icon switches to the active variant AND the log shows 'HotkeyPressed state=Idle'.
Speak a short phrase (~2 seconds).
Press ⌥Space again — confirm the icon stays active (Processing) AND the log shows 'HotkeyPressed state=Recording'.
Wait for the pipeline to complete — confirm the icon returns to idle and no error alert appears.
Open any text field → ⌘V → confirm the transcribed (and optionally LLM-processed) text is pasted.
Click Quit YapTap → confirm the process exits and `ls ~/.config/yaptap/yaptap.lock` returns 'No such file'.

issues.title: Smoke test: file-based hotkey change takes effect after restart
issues.description: The hotkey is already read from ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml at launch and fully wired through config.rs → AppConfig.hotkey → parse_hotkey() → rdev callback. This task verifies the complete round-trip: edit the file, restart, confirm the new key fires and the old key no longer does, and confirm the menu label reflects the new value.
checks: With yaptap running, note the current hotkey displayed in the menu (e.g. 'Hotkey: ⌥Space').
Click 'Open Config…' — confirm ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml opens in the default editor.
Change `hotkey = "option+space"` to `hotkey = "cmd+shift+y"`, save the file.
Click 'Quit YapTap', then relaunch: `RUST_LOG=info ./target/debug/yaptap`.
Open the menu — confirm the label now reads 'Hotkey: ⌘⇧Y'.
Press ⌘⇧Y — confirm the log shows 'HotkeyPressed state=Idle' and the icon switches to active.
Press ⌥Space — confirm nothing happens (old hotkey is no longer registered).
Restore `hotkey = "option+space"` in the config file, quit and relaunch to leave the app in the default state.

issues.title: Share ParsedHotkey via Arc<RwLock> so the rdev callback supports live update without restarting the thread
issues.description: The rdev callback currently captures `parsed: ParsedHotkey` by move (app.rs:521 `move |event|`). This means the parsed hotkey is immutable for the lifetime of the rdev thread — changing the hotkey in-app requires stopping and restarting the thread, which rdev::listen() does not support (no cancellation API). Fix: create `let parsed_hotkey = Arc::new(RwLock::new(parsed));` before the thread::spawn, clone it into the rdev callback, and read it via `.read().unwrap()` on each KeyPress. Store a second clone of the Arc in SharedState so the main event loop can update it when the user picks a new hotkey.
checks: Add `parsed_hotkey: Arc<RwLock<ParsedHotkey>>` field to SharedState in app.rs.
In the Ok(parsed) branch of the hotkey-registration block (app.rs:509), wrap parsed: `let hotkey_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(parsed)); shared.parsed_hotkey = Arc::clone(&hotkey_arc);`

Inside the rdev::listen() callback, replace direct reads of `parsed.modifiers` / `parsed.key` with reads through `hotkey_arc.read().unwrap()` — hold the guard for the duration of the key comparison, then drop it.
If Accessibility is denied (the else branch is skipped), initialise `shared.parsed_hotkey` with the parsed default so the field is always valid.
Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo test` — all pass.
Manually verify existing ⌥Space behaviour is unchanged: press hotkey → icon switches to active.

issues.title: Add AppConfig::save_hotkey() for atomic hotkey persistence in config.rs
issues.description: save_prompt() (config.rs:169) already implements the atomic write pattern (write to .tmp, rename into place, EXDEV fallback). save_hotkey() must follow the identical pattern: clone self, set self.hotkey = new_value, serialise, atomic write. This is separate from save_prompt() because both fields can be updated independently and the caller must update config.hotkey in memory separately.
checks: Add `pub fn save_hotkey(&self, hotkey: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>` to impl AppConfig in config.rs, following the same atomic write logic as save_prompt().
The method clones self, sets `clone.hotkey = hotkey.to_string()`, serialises with toml::to_string_pretty, writes to .tmp, renames to config.toml (EXDEV fallback identical to save_prompt).
Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo test` — all pass.

issues.title: Add in-app hotkey change: enable the Hotkey menu item, show an input dialog, validate, apply live, persist
issues.description: The 'Hotkey: ⌥Space' menu item is currently disabled (app.rs:339 `false`). This task makes it interactive: clicking it opens an NSAlert with an NSTextField pre-filled with the current hotkey string (e.g. 'option+space'). The user edits the string, clicks OK; the app validates with parse_hotkey(), shows an error alert on invalid input, and on success: (1) updates shared.parsed_hotkey via RwLock so the rdev thread picks up the new combo immediately, (2) calls config.save_hotkey() to persist, (3) updates config.hotkey in memory, (4) calls rebuild_menu() so the label reflects the new hotkey. Depends on P7-I012 (Arc<RwLock<ParsedHotkey>>) and P7-I013 (save_hotkey).
checks: In build_menu() (app.rs), change the hotkey MenuItem from `MenuItem::new(&hotkey_text, false, None)` to `MenuItem::new(&hotkey_text, true, None)` and insert `MenuAction::ChangeHotkey` into actions for its id.
Add `ChangeHotkey` variant to the MenuAction enum.

In the MenuEvent handler, add a `MenuAction::ChangeHotkey` arm that calls a new `prompt_hotkey_change(current: &str) -> Option<String>` helper returning the validated new hotkey string, or None if the user cancelled or input was invalid.

Implement `prompt_hotkey_change()` using NSAlert + NSTextField (objc msg_send!): create an NSAlert, set its message text to 'Enter new hotkey (e.g. option+space, cmd+shift+y):', add an NSTextField accessory view pre-filled with the current hotkey, run the alert modally, read the text field on OK.

After a non-None return from prompt_hotkey_change(): call parse_hotkey() to get the new ParsedHotkey, write it to shared.parsed_hotkey via RwLock write guard, call config.save_hotkey(&new_str), set config.hotkey = new_str.clone(), call rebuild_menu().
If the user types an invalid string and clicks OK: show an error alert 'Invalid hotkey: <value>. Use format option+space or cmd+shift+y.' and make no changes.
Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Click 'Hotkey: ⌥Space' in the menu → dialog appears pre-filled with 'option+space'.
Type 'cmd+shift+y' → OK → menu label updates to 'Hotkey: ⌘⇧Y' → press ⌘⇧Y → icon switches to active.
Type an invalid value (e.g. 'win+r') → OK → error alert appears → old hotkey still works.
Click Cancel → no change.
Relaunch the app → confirm the new hotkey persists (was saved to config.toml).
